title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Randy Moss set an NFL record with 23 touchdowns in a single season after being traded to the Patriots from the Raiders.
Wes Welker, traded from the Dolphins in 2007, became a prolific slot receiver but did not win a Super Bowl with the Patriots.
Josh Gordon, acquired in 2018, showed promise but off-field issues limited his impact despite recording over 700 yards in 11 games.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora was ejected in the 8th inning for arguing a check swing strike call.
Cora expressed disappointment that umpire C.B. Bucknor didn't consult the first base umpire on the check swing.
Reds challenged five calls by Bucknor, with all overturned; Red Sox lost both of their challenges early in the game.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story criticized umpire C.B. Bucknor's strike three call, leading to Manager Alex Cora's ejection during the Red Sox loss.
C.B. Bucknor ruled Story had swung, not checking with the first base umpire, creating controversy late in the game.
Despite Story's strikeout, the Red Sox tied the game but ultimately lost 6-5 to finish 1-1 in the series.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha scores 11 goals in 16 games post-Olympics, boosting Bruins' performance.
A concussion kept Zacha from Olympics, motivating his post-break success with Boston.
Zacha achieves career-high 26 goals, expected to surpass personal best 59 points soon.

title: Here’s what Red Sox SP Sonny Gray said about his rough debut
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray allowed four runs in his Red Sox debut, lasting only four innings against the Reds.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora noted defensive shortcomings but praised Gray for settling down after the first inning.
Boston's bullpen held the game, with several pitchers posting scoreless frames until a walk-off hit in the 11th.

title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 150,000 attended the 'No Kings' rally at Boston Common, organized by ACLU of Massachusetts and other groups.
The protest was part of over 3,300 nationwide rallies, with Massachusetts hosting more than 160 events.
Governor Maura Healey and Senator Elizabeth Warren were among the speakers addressing political and social issues.

title: Review & setlist: GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: GWAR performed a 90-minute set at House of Blues in Boston, featuring 17 songs including a cover of 'Pink Pony Club.'
The concert included theatrical elements with costumes and substantial fake blood, engaging fans with shock rock antics.
The setlist included tracks like 'Tormentor,' 'Lot Lizard,' and 'El Presidente,' concluding with an encore featuring former President Bill Clinton.

title: Celtics post lengthy injury report ahead of Sunday’s Hornets matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum are among four Celtics players listed as questionable for the game against the Hornets.
Jaylen Brown is day-to-day with left Achilles tendinitis, while Jayson Tatum is managing a right Achilles repair.
Nikola Vucevic is out with a right ring finger fracture; the game will tip off at 6 p.m. Eastern Time Sunday.

title: ‘No Kings’ protest: What protesters said and why they showed up
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Thousands gathered on Boston Common for 'No Kings' protest against monarchy, including veterans, church groups, and costumed individuals.
Protest featured speeches from Gov. Maura Healey, Sen. Elizabeth Warren, and performances by Dropkick Murphys.
Protesters expressed concerns over democracy's future, with some attending for personal reasons, such as veterans honoring fallen family members.

title: Photo scroll: Scenes from Boston’s ‘No Kings’ protest on March 28
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Thousands gathered at Boston Common for the 'No Kings' protest on March 28, 2026.
Governor Maura Healey and Senators Edward Markey and Elizabeth Warren addressed protestors.
Over 160 'No Kings' events took place across Massachusetts as part of a nationwide protest.
